text: In software development, testing plays a crucial role in ensuring quality. Unit tests check individual pieces of code, like functions, to make sure they work correctly on their own. Integration tests, on the other hand, verify that different parts of a system communicate and work together properly. Many developers use a practice called test-driven development, or TDD, where they write a test first, then write just enough code to pass it. This approach can actually save time by catching bugs early. Overall, a good set of tests gives the team confidence to make changes or add new features without worrying about breaking existing functionality.
